title: Don't set hostPort when generating a service
body:
When generating a kube yaml with kube generate, do not set the hostPort in the pod spec if the service flag is set and we are generating a service kind too.

Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
When generating service kind, don't add hostPort to the pod spec.
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *umohnani8* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [umohnani8] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Kube yaml output with the changes will look like:
```
➜  podman git:(port) ✗ ./bin/podman run -d -p 8080:80 httpd
aa46328ef22a5d76e942ce317f268947858eb07d0ea875169f609fe1eeaee80b
➜  podman git:(port) ✗ ./bin/podman kube generate -s aa46
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-4.5.0-dev
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2023-02-14T15:21:56Z"
  labels:
    app: magicalantonelli-pod
  name: magicalantonelli-pod
spec:
  ports:
  - name: "80"
    nodePort: 30213
    port: 80
    targetPort: 80
  selector:
    app: magicalantonelli-pod
  type: NodePort
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2023-02-14T15:21:56Z"
  labels:
    app: magicalantonelli-pod
  name: magicalantonelli-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - image: docker.io/library/httpd:latest
    name: magicalantonelli
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80
   
➜  podman git:(port) ✗ ./bin/podman kube generate aa46   
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-4.5.0-dev
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2023-02-14T15:22:41Z"
  labels:
    app: magicalantonelli-pod
  name: magicalantonelli-pod
spec:
  containers:
  - image: docker.io/library/httpd:latest
    name: magicalantonelli
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80
      hostPort: 8080
```

@vrothberg @benoitf PTAL
